Q3. Which of the following is the most appropriate description of machine learning in AI?
choices: It is a technology that enters specialized knowledge in a particular field into a computer, and has the computer infer by using the knowledge entered.
It is a technology that gives computers the ability to learn in the same way humans naturally learn, such as finding specific patterns from memorized data.
It is a technology that reproduces vital phenomena or evolutionary processes by using computers and machines.
It is a technology to give computers the ability to manage learning materials and learning by using a web system or other information technology so that people can learn interactively.

Which of the following is a merit of migrating an internal business system to a cloud
service?
choices: If an internal business system that requires a very high level of availability is to be migrated to IaaS, all service providers guarantee high availability, so the migration is easy.

If PaaS is to be used, platform management and OS updates are performed by the service provider, so the burden of installation and operation can be reduced.
If SaaS is to be used temporarily for the development or evaluation of an internal business system, a highly flexible development environment can be provided.

Which of the following is the clause that should be inserted into blank ___A___ of
the SQL statement below in order to display the total sales data for each product that sold
for at least $5,000 from the table OrderDetails?
OrderDetails (OrderID, ProductID, UnitPrice, Quantity)
[SQL statement]
SELECT ProductID, SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity)
FROM OrderDetails
___A___
choices: GROUP BY ProductID HAVING SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity) >= 5000;
HAVING SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity) >= 5000;
HAVING SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity) >= 5000 GROUP BY ProductI;
WHERE SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity) >= 5000;

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an appropriate explanation of the granularity of locks?
choices: When large granularity is set for a large number of transactions to update the same data, the number of transactions that can be executed simultaneously increases.
When large granularity is set for all data in a table to be referenced, data referenced from other transactions can remain undisturbed.
When large granularity is set for data to be updated, the waiting times for other transactions increase, and the total throughput declines.
When large granularity is set, the number of data items included increases, and the number of locks that one transaction makes increases.

Which of the following is a merit in using SaaS?
choices: No system access management needs to be performed, and no consideration concerning the procedure for password initialization or a password policy that satisfies complexity requirements is required.

questions.prompt:

The hash value of each file of the contents of the web server is stored and periodically compared with the hash value generated from each file.
The memory usage of the web server is checked periodically to ensure that a buffer overflow has not occurred.
The updated date of each file of the contents of the web server is stored and compared periodically with the updated date of each file.

questions.prompt:

visualizing business processes?

To represent processes from multiple viewpoints, some purpose-specific modeling methods are used, and each model is depicted by using standardized notation rules for object modeling.
To thoroughly represent the functions of a process, the events that occur in response to a single request are described by using the conditional branch format.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an explanation of green procurement?
choices: It refers not only to the fulfillment of the requirements of quality and price but to the preferential purchase of products and services with a lower environmental burden from companies that work toward a reduction in their environmental impact.
It refers to a certificate that enables commercial trading of the green power that is generated from natural energy sources such as solar, biomass, wind power, and geothermal heat.
It refers to international standards concerning environmental labels that are certified by a third party on the basis of certain criteria.
It refers to mass promotion of the details of environmental protection activities and raising funds for environmental protection from investors.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a characteristic of the growth stage in a product life cycle?
choices: Demand decreases, and the withdrawal of some companies is observed. A decision needs to be made regarding whether it is possible to gain a strong foothold at this stage. Migration into an alternative market is also considered.
Demand is occasional, and exploiting new demand is key to success. Conviction for succeeding in a specific target market is necessary.

Demand reaches a peak, and the differentiation of the product and segmentation of the market become clear. The competition between competitors also intensifies, and it becomes important to add new product types and reduce costs.
The market starts to understand the value of the product. Both the product line and distribution channels need to be expanded. This stage sees an increase in sales and also requires investment.

Which of the following is a description of cost-plus pricing?
choices: It determines a competitive price by referring to the industry average and the pricing of competitors.
It determines a price by adding a certain margin to the manufacturing cost and the purchasing cost.
It determines a price that ensures a certain profit margin after all costs are covered on the basis of a target volume of sales.
It determines an appealing price by researching the quality and price that customers accept.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an explanation of Supply Chain Management (SCM)?
choices: Customer information that are collected through various channels is consolidated and utilized to establish closer relationships with customers.
Employees’ skills and behavior characteristics are grasped, and management such as appropriate personnel assignment and evaluation from the perspective of human resources strategy is performed.
Intellectual assets of individuals, such as know-how and experience, are shared across the organization to be used for creative work.

The entire series of business operations, from purchasing and production to sales and distribution, is reexamined both within the company and among companies in view of total optimization in order to shorten delivery times and minimize inventory.
